findings.summary: The open-posture ACL grants departed analyst Susan Wiley read access to every document created after she left, including both annual financial summaries, because grants are built over the whole roster with no employment-date filter.
findings.evidence: foundation.json ends p:susan.wiley's employment on 2020-10-13, yet her acl.json grant lists 'Finance/FY2021 Financial Summary.xlsx' and 'Finance/FY2022 Financial Summary.xlsx' (manifest dates 2022-01-15 and 2023-01-15) plus all three 2021 'Reeves, Miller and Thompson' engagement documents (2021-03-26, 2021-06-23, 2021-08-31) and 'Firm Overview 2021 v3.docx' (2021-07-01). The asymmetry is the tell: new hire Cynthia Ball (start 2020-11-01) reading the 2019 Johnson files is natural on a shared drive, but a former employee whose access keeps accruing to documents created up to two years after she left is not something a real share does, open or not. It is correct by construction (acl.py builds `readable` over every person in foundation.people and, for posture open, grants each of them every manifest path, ignoring employment.start/end and document date), so the org models the departure as a dated fact but the derived ACL overlay is blind to it.

findings.summary: The who-knows-whom half reads organic, not assembled: staffing varies with headcount and tracks the one departure/backfill, mentions are role-appropriate, and each client carries a single named sponsor. Recording as a strength so it is not flattened.
findings.evidence: engagements.json staffs E-2019-001 as {Fletcher, Miller}, E-2020-001 as {Miller, Fletcher, Wiley}, and E-2021-001 as {Ball, Miller, Fletcher}: the two principals carry every engagement because the firm has exactly two, while the analyst seat rotates Wiley -> (departs 2020-10-13) -> Ball, matching roster_churn.departures=1. mention_map.json names Office Manager Michelle Lopez in exactly one document and does it organically ('Michelle Lopez will confirm the site-visit dates with Stephanie Campos's office this week and get the letterhead and engagement file in order', d:0002), and gives Managing Partner Daniel Jones the three engagement-letter signatures plus the firm overview with no engagement participation, matching the charter's 'Managing Partner signs everything that leaves the building.' Each client has one contact (Campos/Foley/Cole), plausible for lean mid-market work. This is the organic half of the dimension and worth preserving.
